title: Decentralized Social Media Based On Ethereum Blockchain and IPFS
venue: International Conference e-Society, e-Learning and e-Technologies

abstract: This paper investigates the transformative influence of blockchain technology, with a specific focus on Ethereum and the InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) in the context of creating a decentralized social network. The objective is to establish a censorship-resistant, resilient model on the Ethereum private blockchain through the utilization of smart contracts and IPFS. It examines the shortcomings of conventional centralized Online Social Networks (OSNs) and the progression towards decentralized online social networks (DOSNs). The paper deliberates on the rationale behind decentralization in social networking, providing insights into decentralized social media, smart contracts, and blockchain technologies. Furthermore, it presents a methodology for incentivizing accurate reporting and discouraging misinformation. The security elements of a smart contract-based application are scrutinized, covering common attack vectors such as reentrancy, race conditions, integer overflow, and denial of service attacks. The strategies include pre-fund transfer state updates, employment of the Safemath library, and implementation of protective design patterns to ensure a robust security framework and the integrity of the decentralized application.
